source.title: Otitis media (acute): antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.population: Children and young people with acute otitis media.
source.setting: Health services caring for children and young people with acute otitis media.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute otitis media; partial for a broad pediatric ear-pain workflow and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

assessment.text: Requires review for rapid worsening or systemic illness and identifies severe infection and acute complications including mastoiditis for hospital assessment.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped mastoiditis and severe systemic-illness concern prompts in children.

source.title: Otitis media (acute): antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.population: Children and young people with acute otitis media.
source.setting: Health services caring for children and young people with acute otitis media.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute otitis media; partial for a broad pediatric ear-pain workflow and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.12–1.1.14

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires review for rapid worsening or systemic illness and identifies severe infection and acute complications including mastoiditis for hospital assessment.

assessment.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
assessment.rationale: Reviewed for adult sudden or rapidly worsening hearing-loss escalation context; no additional generic ear-pain items were mapped from this section.

source.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
source.setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

assessment.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped adult otalgia, discharge, hearing change, vertigo, ear findings, and referral fields.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
source.setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

assessment.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
assessment.rationale: Reviewed for adult audiology assessment boundaries; no additional legacy ear-pain items were mapped beyond the more specific referral section.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

additions.rationale: Reviewed for adult audiology assessment boundaries; no additional legacy ear-pain items were mapped beyond the more specific referral section.

rewrites.final_wording: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.
